Text: Still recalculating arrays? That's why interviews reject you.
Day one of turning you into a logic monster. Today's weapon, sliding window.
You're given an array. They ask for the max sum of three numbers. Most people panic. They re-ad everything.
Again, and again. That's slow and slow fails interviews.
Here's the smarter way. Imagine a window on the array. It has a start. It has an end.
Now listen carefully. When the window moves, only two numbers matter.
One number leaves. One number enters. That's it. So stop recalculating. Take the current sum.
Subtract what left. Add what entered. Boom.
New answer. The middle never changes. So don't touch it. This single trick changes everything.
Time complexity drops from ON times K to ON. One pass. Zero waste. Fixed window. Same logic.
Variable window. Same logic. Expand the front. Shrink the back. Track the best.
Master this rhythm and half of array interviews are already solved.
Sliding window pattern. Remember it. Use it. Win.
